title: Pods, Multi-Container Composition, Lifecycle, Termination, and Debug Containers · Cluster Case: K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits
summary: An accessible status service shares a Pod with initialization and support containers but loses requests during termination and hides its first crash. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s ephemeral debug container limits increment in bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use ephemeral containers and shared process evidence for bounded debugging while preserving image immutability and naming remaining node-level limits.
Design preStop, stop signals, termination grace, endpoint removal, application draining, child cleanup, and forced termination evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.

version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 5, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. Explain phase, container state, restart, sharing, and replacement boundaries. Competency probe: Use ephemeral containers and shared process evidence for bounded debugging while preserving image immutability and naming remaining node-level limits. A defensible solution applies K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-ephemeral-debug-container-limits — Use ephemeral containers and shared process evidence for bounded debugging while preserving image immutability and naming remaining node-level limits.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-pods-lifecycle-composition-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-pods-lifecycle-composition-5
    artifact: pod.yaml
    decision: worked k8s pods lifecycle composition 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Pod
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      initContainers: [schema-check]
      containers: [api, telemetry-sidecar]
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous -c api
    failure: Reject durable-Pod assumptions, unbounded sidecars, swallowed signals, missing previous logs, or debug mutation presented as image repair.
    repair: Assign container ownership, bound startup and shutdown, capture states and exit causes, and prove a clean replacement preserves required behavior.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: An ephemeral debug container can modify the original image and proves the clean replacement will work. This fails the K8s Ephemeral Debug Container Limits contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
